title: Execution Agent — AI-Powered Order Execution Layer
summary: Replace the rule-based fill watcher with a Claude-powered execution agent that reasons about market microstructure before deciding HOW to execute each trade. Sits between the 6-agent signal pipeline and IBKR — receives the trade decision and autonomously manages order type, aggression, size splitting, and fill chasing based on real-time spread, volume, VWAP position, and time-of-day context.
motivation: The current system naively places all entries as limit orders at price×1.002 with fixed step chasing. In live trading this leads to unnecessary slippage on liquid names and missed fills on fast-moving breakouts. Execution quality becomes material once real capital is deployed — a 0.2% execution improvement on $50k trades is $100/trade.
approach: New execution_agent.py module with a single Claude agent call per trade. Agent receives: symbol, direction, size, conviction score, current bid/ask/spread, relative volume, VWAP position, time of day, and market regime. Returns a structured execution plan: {order_type, limit_price, aggression, split_into_n_tranches, timeout_secs, fallback_strategy}. Fill watcher becomes a sub-strategy within the agent's plan rather than a standalone module.
execution_strategies: aggressive_limit: price at ask, short timeout — for high-conviction breakouts
passive_limit: price at bid+spread/2, longer timeout — for mean-reversion entries
twap_split: split large orders into N tranches over T minutes — for illiquid names
market_on_open: route to MOO for pre-market signals
cancel_on_move: cancel if price moves >X% before fill — entry thesis invalidated
phase_gate: Must be complete before Phase 4 (live trading). Rule-based fill watcher covers paper trading adequately. Execution alpha matters when real capital is at risk.
